name: Mirador de Puigferrós - Circular route from Desaigües
name_original: Mirador de Puigferrós - Vuelta circular desde Desaigües
region_intro: Hiking around Duesaigües offers diverse landscapes within the Mountains of the Costa Daurada, characterized by varied terrain including dense forests of pines, oaks, and holm oaks. The region features significant changes in elevation, providing routes for different skill levels. Notable natural features include the Serra de Pradell and proximity to scenic spots like Mirador De Siurana and Panta de Siurana. Best hiking trails around Duesaigües The most popular hiking route is Trails steeped in history in Escornalbou – hiking in Catalonia, a 4.9 miles (7.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 34 minutes to complete. This route passes by the historic Castell Monestir d'Escornalbou. Another top favourite among local hikers is Mirador de Puigferrós - Circular route from Desaigües, a moderate 6.5 miles (10.4 km) path. This circular route offers views from Mirador de Puigferrós. Local hikers also love the Riudecanyes Reservoir – Lavaderos de Duesaigües loop from Riudecanyes, a 6.1 miles (9.8 km) trail leading through the Riudecanyes Reservoir area, often completed in about 2 hours 46 minutes. Hiking around Duesaigües is defined by dense forests of pines, oaks, and holm oaks, varied terrain with significant elevation changes, and the Serra de Pradell. The network offers options for both less strenuous experiences and more challenging routes. The routes in Duesaigües are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1000 reviews. More than 4000 hikers have used komoot to explore Duesaigües's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Duesaigües?
A: There are over 260 hiking routes in the Duesaigües area, catering to various skill levels. This includes 62 easy trails, 167 moderate options, and 39 more challenging routes for experienced hikers.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Duesaigües?
A: The terrain around Duesaigües is quite varied, featuring dense forests of pines, oaks, and holm oaks. You'll encounter significant changes in elevation, especially on longer routes, providing both challenging climbs and rewarding views. The region is part of the Mountains of the Costa Daurada, offering a mix of natural landscapes.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Duesaigües?
A: Yes, Duesaigües offers several easier routes suitable for families. For instance, the "START TRAIL" is a simpler 6km route with minimal elevation gain, connecting Duesaigües with L'Argentera. You can also find 62 easy trails in the area, perfect for a relaxed outing.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Duesaigües?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e953873522/mirador-de-puigferros-circular-route-from-desaiguees" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Puigferrós - Circular route from Desaigües&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 10.4 km path offering great views. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10419575" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;L'Argentera Village – L'Argentera loop from l'Argentera&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 6.75 km trail.

Q: What historical landmarks can I see while hiking in Duesaigües?
A: The region is rich in history. A prominent landmark is the majestic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5137425" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Miquel d'Escornalbou Castle and Monastery&lt;/a&gt;, which is a significant high point along some trails. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1201468936/trails-steeped-in-history-in-escornalbou-hiking-in-catalonia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trails steeped in history in Escornalbou – hiking in Catalonia&lt;/a&gt; specifically passes by this historic site.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots along the trails?
A: Absolutely. The region offers impressive views, particularly from areas like the Serra de Pradell. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e953873522/mirador-de-puigferros-circular-route-from-desaiguees" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Puigferrós - Circular route from Desaigües&lt;/a&gt; is known for its panoramic vistas. Additionally, natural scenic spots such as Mirador De Siurana and Panta de Siurana are nearby and enhance the natural beauty of the area.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Duesaigües?
A: The routes in Duesaigües are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1000 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the dense forests, and the well-maintained paths that offer options for all skill levels.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by water features like reservoirs?
A: Yes, you can find trails that lead through areas with water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10419638" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riudecanyes Reservoir – Lavaderos de Duesaigües loop from Riudecanyes&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 9.8 km trail that takes you through the Riudecanyes Reservoir area.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Duesaigües?
A: The broader Tarragona region, where Duesaigües is located, is recognized for its ideal hiking conditions throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for exploring the diverse landscapes, from forests to mountainous routes.

Q: Are there any easy, paved paths for a more relaxed walk?
A: Yes, for those seeking a less strenuous experience, there are easier options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10419595" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paved path – The Puigferrós Viewpoint loop from l'Argentera&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.5 km route that provides a gentle walk with scenic views.

Q: What kind of natural features define the Duesaigües hiking experience?
A: Hiking in Duesaigües is characterized by its diverse natural environment. You'll traverse dense forests of pines, oaks, and holm oaks, encounter varied terrain with significant elevation changes, and experience the impressive views from the Serra de Pradell. The area offers a rich natural setting for outdoor activities.
tips: Viewpoint from where you can see Sant Miquel d'Escornalbou and the Moletes mountain in the background.

The Castillo Monasterio de Escornalbou de Riudecanyes is a stately mansion from the beginning of the 20th century formed by the remains of two medieval buildings: the castle, built on the remains of a Roman fortress, and the monastery of Sant Miquel.
Old Catalan farmer's house located on the side of the mountain called Les Masies.

waypoints.name: Sant Miquel d'Escornalbou Castle and Monastery
